title: Blood Establishment Licensing Application Form and Instructions - (FRM-0354) - Canada.ca
content:
## Blood Establishment Licensing Application Form and Instructions - (FRM-0354)

## Blood Establishment Licence
The Blood Establishment Licensing (BEL) Application Form and Instructions are used by establishments in Canada that conduct activities requiring a licence under the Blood Regulations.

## Who is required to hold a BEL?
A person who wishes to perform any of the following activities requires a BEL:
- processing allogeneic blood, except as part of a pre-assessed donor program; Note: Under the Blood Regulations, "processing" is defined as any of the following activities: donor suitability assessment; collection; testing; or blood component preparation.
- Note: Under the Blood Regulations, "processing" is defined as any of the following activities: donor suitability assessment; collection; testing; or blood component preparation.
- importing blood; and/or
- testing blood from a pre-assessed donor.
To obtain an electronic copy of the form, please send a request by email to roeb.blood-sang.dgoral@hc-sc.gc.ca .
Any questions about the Blood Establishment Licences can also be sent to roeb.blood-sang.dgoral@hc-sc.gc.ca .

- Inspection Strategy for Blood Establishments (POL-0039) - summary ;
- Risk Classification of Observations made during Inspections of Blood Establishments (GUI-0061) - Summary
